step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("can communication skills with terminally ill patients be taught")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

First, write at most two sentences analyzing the retrieved evidence in relation to the original question. Then, conclude with your clear answer choice in boxed notation.
Communication skills with terminally ill patients can indeed be taught through validated frameworks such as SPIKES and Ask/Tell/Teach/Ask, which emphasize active listening, empathy, and patient-directed dialogue. These tools are supported by extensive educational activities and continuing professional development aimed at enhancing clinician confidence and competence in delivering emotionally nuanced yet structurally sound end-of-life communications.
